what: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: smscollectr R package designed collection, parsing, export meteorological observations transmitted via SMS. built operational use within ANAM-BF (Agence Nationale de la Météorologie du Burkina Faso), field observers send daily measurements structured text messages central Google Sheet. Scope notice package purpose-built specific operational context ANAM-BF: fixed SMS formats, CLIDATA-compatible output schema, Google Sheets-based collection pipeline. general-purpose SMS parsing library. said, core functions deliberately modular can serve starting point adaptation networks, SMS formats, database targets minimal effort.

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: Workflow Two SMS formats supported automatically detected: Format Gauge — rain gauge observations: 200001S, 03-06-2026, 125 Format Agro — agrometeorological station observations: multi-line message station name, full date, key=value pairs parse_sms() returns named list two tibbles — one per format — can written CSV CLIDATA import. Field observers send structured SMS shared number forwards messages Google Sheet. read_sms() reads, validates, parses messages, returns named list $gauge / $agro. write_csv() exports tibble CSV formatted direct ingestion CLIDATA. clean_sheet() wipes sheet clean successful export.

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: Two things need configured using package: Google Sheets authentication Sheet URL. stored securely system credential store never need set .

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: smscollectr authenticate automatically load — must call sms_auth() explicitly start session. Two modes supported: OAuth (interactive — suitable desktop use): Service account (non-interactive — recommended servers automated pipelines): service account never expires requires browser interaction — preferred solution operational deployments.
code: library(smscollectr)  # Run once interactively — opens a browser for Google login. # The token is cached and reused silently in future sessions. sms_auth() library(smscollectr)  # Path to the JSON key file downloaded from Google Cloud Console. # The Sheet must be shared with the service account email. sms_auth(path = "/secure/path/service-account.json")

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: , get_sheet_url() retrieves anywhere code without exposing URL.
code: # Run once — stores the URL in the system keyring (macOS Keychain, # Windows Credential Manager, or Linux Secret Service). # Never saved in plain text, .Renviron, or any script. set_sheet_url("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SHEET_ID/edit")

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
code: library(smscollectr)  # Authenticate (once per session) sms_auth()                                      # OAuth # sms_auth(path = "/secure/path/key.json")      # Service account  # 1. Read and parse SMS entries result <- read_sms(sheet_url = get_sheet_url())  # 2. Export for CLIDATA import — one file per format readr::write_csv(result$gauge, "export_gauge.csv", na = "") readr::write_csv(result$agro,  "export_agro.csv",  na = "")  # 3. Clear the sheet after a successful export clean_sheet(url = get_sheet_url())

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: Valid examples:
code: XXXXXXY, DD-MM-YYYY, ZZZ 200001S, 03-06-2026, 125     → 12.5 mm at station 200001S on 2026-06-03 200002P, 04-06-2026, TR      → trace rainfall at station 200002P

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: Special values (case-insensitive): TR/tr → trace (value = 0, flag = "T"); NT/nt → measured (value = 0); xx/xxx → missing (value = -9999, flag = "M"). Invalid messages silently dropped never appear output.
code: STATION-NAME DD-MM-YYYY Tn= 305 Tx= 438 TnSol= 305 TxSol= 525 T-10= 423 T-20= 406 T-50= 386 Un= 15 Ux= 54 Vent= 03 Inso= 111 e= 211 BAC= 154 PICHE= 192 RA= NT

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: read_sms() returns named list two tibbles:

title: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data
text: Example output:
code:
parse_sms(c(   "200023P, 11-05-2026, 101",   "DEDOUGOU
11-05-2026
Tn= 305
Tx= 438
..." )) #> $gauge #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   eg_gh_id  year month   day time  eg_el_abbreviation value flag #>   200023P   2026     5    11 06:00 RR                  10.1 NA #> #> $agro #> # A tibble: 15 × 8 #>   eg_gh_id  year month   day time  eg_el_abbreviation value flag #>   200054S   2026     5    11 06:00 TMIN                30.5 NA #>   200054S   2026     5    11 06:00 TMAX                43.8 NA #>   ...

what: Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
title: Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
text: Internal helper. Parses multi-line agrometeorological SMS message tidy long-format tibble one row per observed variable. Returns single-row -NA tibble message fails validation parsed.

title: Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
text: txt character(1). single raw SMS string newline-separated lines.

title: Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
text: tibble::tibble() columns eg_gh_id, year, month, day, time, eg_el_abbreviation, value, flag. One row per variable (15 rows complete message). Returns single-row -NA tibble parsing fails.

title: Parse a single agrometeorological SMS into a long tibble — .parse_agro_sms
text: SMS format :
code: STATION-NAME DD-MM-YYYY Tn= 305 Tx= 438 ... RA= NT

what: Parse a single rain gauge SMS into a one-row tibble — .parse_sms
title: Parse a single rain gauge SMS into a one-row tibble — .parse_sms
text: Internal helper. Returns row NAs SMS fails validation parsed.

title: Parse a single rain gauge SMS into a one-row tibble — .parse_sms
Parse a single rain gauge SMS into a one-row tibble — .parse_sms
text: txt character(1). single SMS string.

title: Parse a single rain gauge SMS into a one-row tibble — .parse_sms
text: tibble::tibble() columns eg_gh_id, year, month, day, value, flag. columns NA parsing fails.

what: Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
title: Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
text: Internal helper. Interprets raw string extracted SMS message returns named list physical value quality flag.

title: Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
text: raw character(1). Raw string extracted SMS message. divisor numeric(1). Value divide numeric raw value . Default 10.

title: Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
text: named list two elements: value numeric. Physical value, 0, -9999, NA. flag character. "T" trace, "M" missing, NA otherwise.

title: Parse a raw SMS value into a numeric value and quality flag — .parse_val
text: Recognised special strings (case-insensitive): TR, Tr, tR, tr Trace — returns value = 0, flag = "T". NT, Nt, nT, nt measured — returns value = 0, flag = NA. xx, XX, Xx (xxx variants) Missing — returns value = -9999, flag = "M".

what: Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
title: Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
text: valid agrometeorological SMS starts plain station name line 1, followed full date DD-MM-YYYY format line 2, series Key= value lines.

title: Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
text: text character vector SMS strings check. NULL accepted returns logical(0).

title: Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
text: logical vector length text.

title: Check whether a string is a valid agrometeorological SMS — is_agro_sms
code:
msg <- "DEDOUGOU
11-05-2026
Tn= 305
Tx= 438" is_agro_sms(msg)                          # TRUE #> [1] FALSE is_agro_sms("200001S, 03-06-2026, 125")   # FALSE #> [1] FALSE is_agro_sms(NULL)                         # logical(0) #> logical(0)

what: Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
title: Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
text: valid SMS starts 6-digit station identifier followed one P, S, , C, comma (e.g. "200001S, 03-06-2026, 125").

title: Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
text: text character vector SMS strings check. NULL accepted returns logical(0).

title: Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
text: logical vector length text.

title: Check whether a string is a valid rain gauge SMS — is_gauge_sms
code: is_gauge_sms("200001S, 03-06-2026, 125")  # TRUE #> [1] TRUE is_gauge_sms("hello world")               # FALSE #> [1] FALSE is_gauge_sms(NULL)                        # logical(0) #> logical(0)

what: Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
title: Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
text: Detects format SMS message, dispatches appropriate parser, returns named list two tibbles — one per format.

title: Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
text: texts character vector raw SMS strings.

title: Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
text: named list two tibble::tibble() elements: gauge Parsed rain gauge observations columns eg_gh_id, year, month, day, time, eg_el_abbreviation, value, flag. agro Parsed agrometeorological observations columns. tibble empty (correctly typed) valid messages format found.

title: Parse a vector of SMS messages (all formats) — parse_sms
text: Supported formats: gauge Format — Rain gauge: "200001S, 03-06-2026, 125". agro Format B — Agrometeorological: multi-line [STATION]\n[DD]\nKey= val. Invalid unrecognised messages silently dropped. Duplicates (eg_gh_id, year, month, day, eg_el_abbreviation) keep last occurrence within tibble.

what: Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
title: Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
text: Reads SMS messages Google Sheet column, parses structured rainfall observations, deduplicates station date, formats result ingestion climatological database.

title: Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
text: sheet_url character(1). Full URL Google Sheet. sheet character(1) integer(1). Sheet name index. Default 1 (first sheet). col character(1) integer(1). Name index column containing SMS text. Default "sms".

title: Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
text: tibble::tibble() columns: eg_gh_id character. Station identifier. eg_el_abbreviation character. Element code, always "RR" (rainfall). month character. Zero-padded month ("01" "12"). day character. Zero-padded day ("01" "31"). value numeric. Rainfall mm. flag character. "T" trace rainfall, NA otherwise. Returns empty tibble correct column types valid SMS messages found.

title: Read and parse SMS rain gauge entries from a Google Sheet — read_sms
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SHEET_ID/edit" read_sms(url) read_sms(url, sheet = "Saisie", col = "message") } # }

what: Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
title: Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
text: Saves Google Sheet URL securely using system keyring (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service). URL never written disk plain text, .Renviron, R script.

title: Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
text: url character(1). Full Google Sheet URL store. key character(1). Key name used retrieve URL later. Default "gs_url". service character(1). Keyring service namespace. Default "smscollectr".

title: Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
text: Invisibly returns key.

title: Store a Google Sheet URL in the system credential store — set_sheet_url
Authenticate with Google Sheets — sms_auth
text: Wraps googlesheets4::gs4_auth(). Call per session using Sheet-related functions. Uses service account JSON provided, otherwise falls back OAuth persistent cache.

title: Authenticate with Google Sheets — sms_auth
Authenticate with Google Sheets — sms_auth
text: path character(1) NULL. Path service account JSON key file. NULL, OAuth used. email character(1) NULL. Google account email OAuth. cache character(1). Path OAuth token cache directory. Default "~/.smscollectr_secrets".

title: Authenticate with Google Sheets — sms_auth
text: Invisibly returns TRUE.

what: smscollectr: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data — smscollectr-package
title: smscollectr: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data — smscollectr-package
text: Ingests, parses, exports rain gauge SMS messages Google Sheets. Supports incremental reading, CLICOM output, FTP delivery.

title: smscollectr: Collect and Process Rain Gauge SMS Data — smscollectr-package
text: Maintainer: Ousmane Ouedraogo ousmane@anam.bf

what: Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
title: Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
text: Map TCM variable name CLIDATA element abbreviation

title: Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
text: x character. Internal TCM variable name(s) (e.g. "TMIN", "TS-10", "WDD").

title: Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
text: character vector CLIDATA element abbreviations (eg_el_abbreviation). Returns NA warning unrecognised names.

title: Map a TCM variable name to its CLIDATA element abbreviation — to_element_id
code: to_element_id("Tn")    # "TMIN" #> [1] "TMIN" to_element_id("T-10")   # "TS-10" #> [1] "TS-10" to_element_id("UNKNOWN") # NA + warning #> Warning: Unrecognised element name(s): UNKNOWN #> [1] NA

what: Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
title: Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
text: Map station name CLIDATA station code

title: Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
text: x character. Station name(s) appear TCM SMS. Matching case-insensitive strips surrounding whitespace.

title: Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
text: character vector CLIDATA station codes (eg_gh_id). Returns NA warning unrecognised names.

title: Map a station name to its CLIDATA station code — to_station_id
code: to_station_id("DORI")              # "200026S" #> [1] "200026S" to_station_id("bobo-dioulasso")    # "200099S" #> [1] "200099S" to_station_id("UNKNOWN")           # NA + warning #> Warning: Unrecognised station name(s): UNKNOWN #> [1] NA
